payload.description: Design and implement a native cross-platform guided init wizard with Questionary and Rich while preserving non-interactive compatibility.

checklist.note: The implementation plan weakens the design's mandatory native-Windows interactive smoke requirement.
The proposed lazy loader cannot resolve leaf commands with the existing rewrap_subtree contract.
The declared files cover the proposed implementation and verification work.

findings.summary: LazyGroup.get_command unconditionally calls rewrap_subtree(command), but the existing helper accepts a Click Group and accesses command.commands. Resolving a leaf such as init_cmd will raise AttributeError before dispatch; the plan needs explicit leaf/group handling and coverage for a real leaf command.

The plan permits a Windows CI receipt to replace the native Windows interactive smoke run, contradicting the design's requirement to manually exercise real arrow, Space, Enter, Back, cancel, ASCII fallback, and a spaced path. The change could therefore be declared complete without validating its primary guided Windows interaction contract.

checklist.note: Ctrl+C handling and the final review's file-action claims violate the approved design.
All changed files are within the declared implementation-plan scope.
GitHub append applies stale snapshot bytes without validating the current file.
Pre-apply Ctrl+C and concurrent PR-template modification are unsafe.
Getting-started claims existing AGENTS.md is shown as Update, but the generated plan reports Preserve.

findings.summary: Questionary's `ask()` catches KeyboardInterrupt and returns None, which this adapter maps to explicit cancellation. Consequently Ctrl+C in the real guided UI exits successfully as “Setup cancelled” instead of propagating to Click for the required exit 1. The fake-adapter test does not exercise Questionary's swallowing behavior.

Existing AGENTS.md and .gitignore default to FileAction.PRESERVE, rendered as “will be left unchanged,” but the executor unconditionally updates both managed sections. The final confirmation therefore misrepresents user-owned file writes and contradicts the documented representative plan.

APPEND constructs output from cached inspection bytes without re-reading or comparing the current PR template. Edits made after inspection, including during the new review stage, are silently overwritten; the supposed “changed after inspection” guard only parses the stale snapshot and cannot detect this condition.

checklist.note: codex: GitHub append applies stale snapshot bytes without validating the current file.
codex: Getting-started claims existing AGENTS.md is shown as Update, but the generated plan reports Preserve.
codex: Pre-apply Ctrl+C and concurrent PR-template modification are unsafe.
codex: All changed files are within the declared implementation-plan scope.
codex: Ctrl+C handling and the final review's file-action claims violate the approved design.

findings.summary: APPEND constructs output from cached inspection bytes without re-reading or comparing the current PR template. Edits made after inspection, including during the new review stage, are silently overwritten; the supposed “changed after inspection” guard only parses the stale snapshot and cannot detect this condition.

Existing AGENTS.md and .gitignore default to FileAction.PRESERVE, rendered as “will be left unchanged,” but the executor unconditionally updates both managed sections. The final confirmation therefore misrepresents user-owned file writes and contradicts the documented representative plan.

Questionary's `ask()` catches KeyboardInterrupt and returns None, which this adapter maps to explicit cancellation. Consequently Ctrl+C in the real guided UI exits successfully as “Setup cancelled” instead of propagating to Click for the required exit 1. The fake-adapter test does not exercise Questionary's swallowing behavior.

checklist.note: The plan does not preserve the specified explicit-model discovery bypass.
Malformed provider configuration has contradictory write-boundary semantics.
The dependency lockfile is omitted despite planned runtime dependency changes.

findings.summary: Interactive preflight unconditionally calls discover_reviewer_models for every provider, so an explicit --review-model still opens provider configuration. This contradicts the design requirement that explicit values bypass discovery; the later UI gate only bypasses selection, not configuration access.

The compatibility contract says malformed or unreadable provider configuration must write nothing, while the proposed UI disables that reviewer and continues with human review, allowing initialization writes. The design must choose and test one mutation boundary.

The declared scope omits uv.lock even though Tasks 5 and 11 add runtime dependencies to pyproject.toml. This repository tracks uv.lock for reproducible installs, so the plan's file gate prevents synchronizing the dependency lock.

checklist.note: The implementation plan omits a required guided GitHub setup choice.
The task sequence contains contradictory reviewer-model milestones.
The declared file scope covers the planned production, test, CI, dependency, and documentation changes.

findings.summary: The design requires guided users to choose whether to configure GitHub when the flag has not enabled it, but no UI task or public-command test collects that choice; setup_github is only copied from the Click flag. The required Stage 2 choice therefore cannot be implemented or reviewed before writes.

Task 5 requires interactive reviewer-model selection without free-text and expects all UI tests to pass, but model discovery is not introduced until Tasks 11–13, where Task 13 explicitly expects both backends still to use free-text. The sequential plan cannot satisfy both milestones as written.
